title: Quality
summary: A discipline that forces the model to programmatically verify claims and show real evidence — command output, test runs, diffs, queries, and for anything visual a screenshot / preview / short recording — instead of asserting "done". Evidence must be real; never mocked, edited, or described without being produced.
problem: LLMs declare success without checking, and "done ✅" hides broken work. This makes verification mandatory and the proof visible before anything is reported as working, fixed, or passing.

skills.name: prove-it-validation
skills.purpose: Before claiming any task succeeded, verify it programmatically and show the evidence (output, tests, diffs, screenshots/recordings) — never assert success on faith.
